phases.name: Foundation
phases.description: Walking skeleton: hook -> server -> SQLite -> minimal dashboard. Proves architecture end-to-end.

steps.title: Project scaffold and core types
steps.description: Initialize pnpm workspace with 7 packages. Define domain types as discriminated unions: HookEvent (7 variants), Session, AgentNode, McpServerHealth. Configure TypeScript project references enforcing dependency rules.
acceptance_criteria: Monorepo builds successfully with all 7 packages
Core package has zero runtime dependencies
Domain event types cover all 7 hook event variants
TypeScript project references enforce inward dependency rule
architectural_constraints: Core module imports nothing from other packages
All types defined as readonly (immutable domain)

steps.title: Storage port and SQLite adapter with schema
steps.description: Define storage port as function-signature types. Implement SQLite adapter with better-sqlite3. Create initial schema (events, sessions, mcp_events, agent_spans tables). Schema versioning via migration runner.
acceptance_criteria: Storage port defined as record of function types using core domain types
SQLite adapter implements all port functions
Schema creates 4 tables with appropriate indexes
Migration runner applies pending migrations on startup
architectural_constraints: Port definition uses only core types
SQLite adapter is the only module importing better-sqlite3
WAL mode enabled for concurrent read access

steps.title: Event ingress server and hook scripts
steps.description: Fastify server with POST /api/events endpoint. JSON Schema validation of incoming hook events. Event processor transforms raw events to domain events, persists via storage port. Hook script templates for .claude/settings.json. norbert init command writes hooks and starts server.
acceptance_criteria: Server accepts POST /api/events and persists valid events
Malformed events rejected with 400 status
norbert init writes 7 hook entries to .claude/settings.json additively
Initialization is atomic (no partial state on failure)
Health endpoint responds at /health
architectural_constraints: Hook scripts are async fire-and-forget (non-blocking to Claude Code)
Server receives storage port via dependency injection (not direct import)

steps.title: Minimal dashboard and walking skeleton integration
steps.description: SvelteKit app serving on localhost via Norbert server. Minimal event list page showing captured events. WebSocket connection for real-time updates. norbert serve and norbert status commands.
acceptance_criteria: Dashboard displays captured events with timestamp, tool name, status
WebSocket pushes new events to dashboard without page refresh
norbert status shows server state, event count, MCP servers seen
First event visible on dashboard within 5 minutes of install
Server crash does not affect Claude Code operation
architectural_constraints: Dashboard communicates with server via REST API and WebSocket only
Dashboard has zero runtime imports from other Norbert packages

phases.name: Data Pipeline
phases.description: Comprehensive event capture for all hook types with MCP attribution and agent tree construction.

steps.title: Full event capture pipeline with MCP attribution
steps.description: Expand event processing for all 7 hook types. Extract MCP attribution (server, tool name) from tool events. Build session aggregates (token counts, agent count, cost estimates). Construct agent parent-child relationships from SubagentStart/SubagentStop pairs.
acceptance_criteria: All 7 hook event types captured and stored with correct fields
MCP tool calls include server name and tool name
Built-in tool calls stored with null MCP fields
Parent-child agent relationships queryable from agent_spans
Events during server downtime do not corrupt existing data
Session aggregates update incrementally on each event
architectural_constraints: Event processing functions are pure (in core module)
MCP attribution uses OTel-aligned field names
Raw event payload preserved in raw_payload column

phases.name: Dashboard Core Views
phases.description: Overview page, execution trace graph, and token cost waterfall -- the three primary dashboard experiences.

steps.title: Dashboard overview with summary cards and MCP health
steps.description: Replace minimal event list with overview page: today's summary cards (sessions, tokens, cost, MCP servers), recent sessions table (sortable, clickable), MCP server health table (status, calls, errors, token overhead). Empty state guidance for new installs.
acceptance_criteria: Summary cards show session count, total tokens, estimated cost, MCP server count
Sessions table sorted by start time, shows agents/tokens/cost/MCP errors/duration
MCP health table shows per-server status, call count, errors, token overhead
Dashboard loads in under 2 seconds with 100 sessions
Empty state shows helpful guidance
architectural_constraints: API endpoints serve pre-computed aggregates (not raw event queries)
MCP servers with errors are visually highlighted

steps.title: Execution trace graph with interactive DAG
steps.description: Session detail page with D3.js DAG visualization of agent topology. Nodes show agent name, cost, tool count. Edges show parent-child delegation. Nodes expandable to show individual tool calls. Failed agents show error indicators. Single-agent sessions display cleanly.
acceptance_criteria: DAG renders correct parent-child agent relationships
Each node shows agent name, token cost, tool call count, duration
Clicking a node reveals individual tool calls within that agent
Failed agents display error indicator with error details
Single-agent sessions render without broken layout
Graph renders in under 3 seconds for 20 agents
architectural_constraints: Trace building is a pure function in core module
DAG visualization uses D3.js (no framework-specific graph library)

steps.title: Token cost waterfall with MCP attribution
steps.description: Cost waterfall view in session detail page. Per-agent breakdown (input tokens, output tokens, cost). MCP tool calls attributed to server (server:tool format). Agents sorted by cost descending. Expandable for per-tool-call detail. Cost estimation footnote.
acceptance_criteria: Per-agent cost breakdown with input/output tokens and estimated cost
Agents sorted by cost descending
Expanding agent shows per-tool-call breakdown
MCP tool calls show server:tool_name format
Agent costs sum to within 5% of session total
Cost estimation methodology footnote visible
architectural_constraints: Cost calculation is a pure function in core module
Cost rates configurable via config (not hardcoded)

phases.name: MCP Observatory
phases.description: Dedicated MCP health panel with error timeline, latency tracking, and diagnostic recommendations.

steps.title: MCP health dashboard with error timeline and diagnostics
steps.description: Dedicated MCP panel: real-time connection status per server, historical event timeline (connect/disconnect/reconnect), error categorization (connection/timeout/registration/silent drop), tool call explorer with per-server attribution and latency, progressive latency degradation detection, diagnostic recommendations per error type. Empty state for users with no MCP servers.
acceptance_criteria: Per-server connection status (connected/disconnected/error) displayed
Historical timeline shows connect/disconnect/reconnect events
Errors categorized by type with diagnostic recommendations
Tool call explorer shows server name, tool, latency, and success/fail
Latency degradation pattern detection and warning
Empty state for no MCP servers shows helpful explanation
architectural_constraints: MCP analysis functions are pure (in core module)
Connection status inferred from event patterns (no direct health check)
Latency computed from PreToolUse to PostToolUse timestamp delta

phases.name: Optimization Loop
phases.description: Session comparison and history -- closing the observe-diagnose-optimize feedback loop.

steps.title: Session comparison (CLI and dashboard)
steps.description: Compare two sessions side-by-side: tokens, cost, per-agent breakdown, MCP errors, duration. Change percentages computed. Projected monthly savings estimated. CLI: norbert cost --last --compare. Dashboard: comparison view. Handle differing agent configurations (new/removed agents).
acceptance_criteria: Side-by-side metrics for tokens, cost, agents, MCP errors, duration
Change percentages computed and displayed
New/removed agents labeled appropriately
Projected monthly savings computed from cost delta and frequency
Helpful message when fewer than 2 sessions exist
architectural_constraints: Comparison logic is a pure function in core module
Available in both CLI and dashboard

steps.title: Session history with search, trends, and CSV export
steps.description: Session history page: searchable/filterable by date, cost, agent count. Weekly/monthly cost trend charts. Computed baselines (avg cost, P95, avg duration). CSV export for stakeholder reporting. Preliminary baselines with confidence note for insufficient data.
acceptance_criteria: Session list filterable by date range, cost range, agent count
Weekly cost trend chart with daily granularity
Baselines computed: average cost, P95 cost, average duration
CSV export containing date, sessions, tokens, cost
Insufficient data shows preliminary baselines with confidence note
Session list sortable by cost, date, duration, agent count
architectural_constraints: Trend data computed from SQLite aggregates
CSV export generated server-side from query results

in_scope: Hook-based data capture for 7 Claude Code lifecycle events
Local SQLite storage with OTel-aligned schema
CLI companion for quick queries and server management
Token cost waterfall with MCP attribution
MCP server health dashboard with error timeline
Session comparison and history with CSV export
out_of_scope_mvp: MCP token overhead analyzer detail view (Phase 2)

quality_gates: All 8 user stories traceable to roadmap steps
Architecture tests enforce module dependency rules in CI
Core module has zero runtime dependencies
Dashboard module has zero runtime Norbert imports
Walking skeleton (Phase 01) validates full pipeline before feature work
Cost estimates within 5% of session totals
